QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_19

question.text: What must the offer document on the websites of SEBI, lead managers, and stock exchanges match?

answer.text: The summary version submitted to the stock exchanges for listing purposes
The latest amended version incorporating all SEBI observations
The printed copies filed with the RoC, SEBI, and stock exchanges
The draft version originally filed, without any subsequent modifications

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The offer document made available on websites must be an exact match of the printed copies filed with the Registrar of Companies (RoC), SEBI, and stock exchanges. This ensures consistency — an investor reading the online version gets the same information as one reading the physical copy.
**The Trap:** "Latest amended version incorporating all SEBI observations" sounds logical but is imprecise — the regulation specifically requires matching the filed printed copies, which would already incorporate SEBI's observations if they were addressed before filing.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests the concept of document consistency across platforms. This principle extends to mutual fund offer documents (SID/SAI) that must be consistent across AMFI/AMC websites and physical copies.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Before the issue opens, download the offer document from each website (SEBI, lead manager, exchanges) and verify page counts and key sections match the RoC-filed copy. Any discrepancy is a compliance risk.
